rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly as a core prior study on conceptual and perceptual fluency effects on brand evaluation, not just as background. The citing paper explicitly positions its contribution as extending that line to regulatory goal fluency, and it draws on specific evidence and examples from Lee and Labroo (2004). It also adapts stimuli from Lee and Labroo (2004, Study 4) and interprets new findings against that prior result, offering a revised account for a negative-prime effect. These roles show a direct theoretical and empirical build-on.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Lee and Labroo (2004) by introducing regulatory goal fluency as an additional fluency mechanism affecting brand evaluation after related ad exposure. It uses the earlier conceptual/perceptual fluency framework and adapted experimental materials as a direct springboard for its hypotheses and tests.
